Schengen Area.description: 29 European countries with abolished internal border controls. A single Schengen visa covers all member states. Non-EU visitors are limited to 90 days within any 180-day period.

European Union.description: A political and economic union of 27 European member states. EU citizens have the right to live, work and travel freely across all member states.

Common Travel Area.description: An open borders arrangement between the United Kingdom and Ireland. British and Irish citizens can move freely between the two countries without passport checks.

Gulf Cooperation Council.description: A political and economic union of six Arab Gulf states, founded in 1981. GCC citizens enjoy free movement and shared visa policies across member states.

ASEAN.description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations — a regional intergovernmental organisation of 11 Southeast Asian countries promoting economic, political and security cooperation.

CARICOM.description: The Caribbean Community — a political and economic union of 15 member states across the Caribbean. CARICOM members generally enjoy freedom of movement among member states.

ECOWAS.description: The Economic Community of West African States — a regional bloc of 12 West African countries (as of 2025, following the withdrawal of Mali, Burkina Faso and Niger). ECOWAS citizens can move freely across member states.
aliases: Economic Community of West African States

East Africa Tourist Visa.description: A joint tourist visa covering Kenya, Uganda and Rwanda. A single visa grants entry to all three countries, valid for 90 days.

KAZA Visa.description: The Kavango–Zambezi (KAZA) UniVisa covers Zambia and Zimbabwe. A single visa grants entry to both countries and allows day trips to Botswana via the Kazungula border post.

Mercosur.description: A South American trade bloc whose full members are Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay. Citizens of member and associate states enjoy simplified border crossing arrangements.

Nordic Countries.description: Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway and Sweden share a long history of cooperation and free movement under the Nordic Passport Union, predating the Schengen Area.

Pacific Islands Forum.description: An intergovernmental organisation of 18 countries across the Pacific region, promoting cooperation on trade, security and development.

Commonwealth of Nations.description: A political association of 56 member states, mostly former territories of the British Empire. Commonwealth citizens may receive consular assistance from other member states where their own country has no embassy.
